name: Peanut Chutney
description: Peanut Chutney, also known as Groundnut Chutney, Verkadalai Chutney or Palli Chutney, is a flavourful nut-based side dish from Andhra and other Southern parts of India. Roasted peanuts are ground with onions, garlic, chillies, tamarind and jaggery to create a creamy, nutty and spicy chutney. It is quick to make and tastes amazing with idli, dosa, punugulu or vada.

steps.instruction: If using raw peanuts, dry roast them on low flame for 4 to 5 minutes until aromatic and golden. Remove skins if needed.
steps.tip: Always use roasted peanuts for this chutney. Raw peanuts will not give the right flavour or texture.

steps.instruction: Add roasted peanuts, onion, garlic, dry red chillies, tamarind, jaggery and salt to a blender jar.
Add ¼ cup water and grind to a smooth or slightly coarse paste. Add more water as needed for consistency.
Transfer the peanut chutney to a serving bowl.
For tempering, heat oil in a small pan. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add urad dal and fry until golden.
Add curry leaves and asafoetida. Fry for a few seconds until leaves are crisp.
Pour the tempering over the chutney and mix well.
Serve with idli, dosa, vada, paniyaram or punugulu.
steps.tip: This chutney stays good in the refrigerator for 2 to 3 days.

tips: Always use roasted peanuts for this chutney. Raw peanuts will not give the right flavour or texture.
If using store-bought roasted peanuts, check that they do not contain corn syrup or additives.
Adjust the number of red chillies based on your spice preference.
The chutney has a creamy yet nutty mouthfeel — similar to a spicy peanut sauce.
You can skip onion and garlic for a simpler version, or add grated coconut for extra richness.
This chutney stays good in the refrigerator for 2 to 3 days.
